synonyms: Copaiva. -THE GUIDING SYMPTOMS OF OUR MATERIA MEDICA By CONSTANTINE HERING, M.D

generalities.text: Main THE ENCYCLOPEDIA OF PURE MATERIA MEDICA By TIMOTHY F. ALLEN, A.M., M.D. Presented by Médi-T COPAIVA. Copaifera officinalis, L. Natural order , Leguminosæ. Preparation , Tincture of the balsam. Authorities. 1 , Hahnemann, Fragmenta de Vir.; 2 , Teste, provings on self and seven or eight persons of both sexes, with the 6th dil., System. de la Mat. Méd.; 3 , Ansiaux, Clinique Chir., Liège, 1816, from Roth, M. M.; 4 , Ribes, Bull. d. l. Soc. d'émulation, 1822, from Roth; 5 , Delpech, Mem. sur l. baume de Copahu, from Roth; 6 , Roemhild, Preuss. Ver., from Roth; 7 , Grossheim, from the same; 8 , Loerenhardt, from the same; 9 , Merat and Dolens, Dict. de M. Méd., from Roth; 10 , Lisle, Rec. de Mém. de Méd., from Roth; 11 , Fox, effect on the skin, Lancet, 1867; 12 , Hardy, Gaz. des Hôp (N. Y. Med J., 10, 416), effect on skin when administered for gonorrha; 13 , Sachse, Med. Zeit., 1839, effects when taken for gonorrha; 14 , (omitted); 15 , Mitscherlich, general poisoning symptoms, Pharm. J., 9, 233; 16 , Konig, a student, took repeated doses of an emulsion of half an ounce of balsam with four ounces of "vehicle" (two spoonful doses), Wibmer; 17 , Schroff, general effects, Lehrb. d. Pharm.; 18 , Dr. Behn, effects on skin of patients taking Cop., from Schroff; 19 , Husemann. Pflanzenstoffe effects; 20 , Lehmann, effects on patients, from Husemann; 21 , Weil, general statement of effects, A. H. Z., 84, 41; 22 , Lembke, proving with 10 to 70 drops, Z. fur H. Kl., 11, 161. MIND. ► Emotional. ► Unconquerable dislike for his usual employment, [2] . ► Disgust with life, and at the same time fear of death, [2] . ► During the menses, sadness in the morning, depression, pains in the loins, and extreme excitement of the nervous system, [2] . ► In the morning, soon after waking, deep sadness, which ceases during a walk, but returns in the evening, [2] . ► Periodical attacks in the afternoon of sadness and weeping, with coldness of the extremities, and hot flushes in the face, [2] . ► A young girl bursts into tears on hearing the sound of a piano, [2] . ► Anxiety about his health, [2] . ► Peevish, irascible, morose, intolerable temper during a whole week, with ebullition of the blood, heat of the head, and trembling of the hands from the least contradiction, [2] . ► Absurd recriminations about trifling and long-past occurrences, [2] . [10.] ► Misanthropy, [2] . ► Intellectual. ► Entire unfitness for study; the head is empty and the ideas are confused; dull pain in the forehead when striving against this, [2] . ► Defective memory, which vexes him, and finally throws him into a state of gloomy discouragement, [2] . HEAD. ► Vertigo. ► Vertigo and headache, [3] . ► Attack of vertigo, when riding on horseback, in the forenoon, although he was talking at the time and walking the horse, [2] . ► Transient attacks of vertigo when sitting, working, walking, but especially when standing upright and motionless, [2] . ► General Head. ► Rush of blood to the head and face during and after eating, [2] . ► Heaviness of the head, and especially of the occipital region, which is instinctively pressed against the collar of the coat; this affords relief, [2] . ► Headache in the forenoon, [2] . ► Forehead. ► Pressure at the forehead, [2] . ► Temples. [20.] ► Stitches in the temples and behind the ears, [2] . ► Sudden stitches in both temples when washing the face with cool water in the morning, [2] . ► Tearing pain in the temporal regions, especially in the left, alleviated by gentle pressure with the hand, [2] . ► Bruised pain in the right temple, evening and night, becoming intolerable when the affected part is pressed against the pillow, [2] . ► Vertex. ► Pulsating stitches at the top of the head, not synchronous with the pulse, [2] . ► Parietals. ► Hemicrania (on the left side), with boring pain, sense of coldness in the affected part, weeping, and constant moaning fro three days, in a young man of twenty-two years, disposed to hypochondria. (The pain was relieved by Mercurius ), [2] . ► Occiput. ► Dull pain in the occiput, [2] . ► Lancinating pressure at the right occipital protuberance, [2] . ► Pulsating deepseated stitches in the occiput, [2] . ► Stitches in the left occipital protuberance, with occasional shocks in the whole head, only in the morning, [2] . ► External Head. [30.] ► Falling of the hair after the first doses, [2] . ► Sensitiveness of the scalp, and even of the hair, [2] . EYE. ► Objective. ► Redness of the left eye, [2] . ► Lids. ► Spasm of the right upper lid, recurring several times in the daytime, and accompanied by a slight pressive pain in the eyes, [2] . ► Involuntary contraction of the orbicularis muscle of the eyes in the morning, [2] . ► Agglutination of the lids in the morning, [2] . ► Pupil. ► Contraction of the pupils, [2] . ► Vision. ► Sensitiveness of the eyes to the light, [2] . ► Transient obscuration of sight, [2] . ► Upon alternately closing the right and the left eye, the same objects look paler when seen with the latter than when seen with the former, [2] . [40.] ► Black points hovering before the eyes, [2] . EAR. ► Stitches for a whole night in the left ear, causing one to cry out; some days after this a little purulent matter is discharged from the meatus (after three days), [2] . ► Formication in the ear, [2] . ► Hearing. ► Excessive sensitiveness of hearing, especially as to sharp sounds, to such a degree that the report of a pistol caused an extremely disagreeable and even painful sensation; this sensation did not arise from the detonation of the powder, but from the impact of the ball against the metallic disk at which it was fired, [2] . ► Painful reverberation of every sound in the head, ceasing after half an hour's walk, [2] . ► Humming in the ears, [2] . ► Buzzing in the ears, [2] . NOSE. ► Frequent sneezing, [2] . ► Stoppage of the nose for two days, only in the morning, [2] . ► Fluent coryza, with headache, pressive pain at the root of the nose, and itching in the eyes, [2] . FACE. [50.] ► Paleness and sickly look of the face, [2] . ► Bloating of the face, [2] . ► Transient tearings in the left cheek, [2] . MOUTH. ► Teeth. ► An unpleasant smell seems to come from the teeth, [2] . ► The teeth seem less firm, and are loose in their sockets, [2] . ► The teeth are on edge, [2] . ► Sensation as if the teeth were elongated, [2] . ► Sense of coldness in the teeth, [2] . ► Gnawing and throbbing pain in a carious tooth, worse when drawing in cool air, or by the contact of cold drink, [2] . ► Gums. ► Excoriating pain in the gums, palate, and pharynx, [2] . ► Tongue. [60.] ► Redness, with smarting, on the sides and at the tip of the tongue, [2] . ► *The tongue is covered with a whitish coat , green at the base, [2] . ► General Mouth. ► Dryness of the mouth, especially at night and in the morning, [2] . ► Tenacious phlegm in the mouth and throat, which is constantly reproduced, [2] . ► Saliva. ► Transiently increased secretion of saliva, [16] . ► Flow of saliva in the evening, at night, and in the morning, [2] . ► Every now and then sudden and profuse flow of sweetish saliva, [2] . ► Taste. ► Bitter taste in the mouth, [2] . ► Every article of food seems too salt, [2] . THROAT. ► Some pains in the throat, without the slightest swelling of the tonsils or fauces, [20] . [70.] ► Constriction of the throat, [2] . ► Tonsils. ► Swelling of both tonsils (especially of the right), [2] . ► Pharynx. ► Sensation as of a foreign body in the pharynx, [2] . ► Troublesome pressure in the pharynx, [2] . ► External Throat. ► Sensitiveness without perceptible swelling of the parotid glands, but bruised pain and evident swelling of the submaxillary glands, [2] . STOMACH. ► Appetite. ► Unwonted hunger in the evening, when on the point of going to bed, [2] . ► Excessive hunger (the first two days), then loss of appetite, [2] . ► Loss of appetite, the whole second day, [16] . ► Loathing of food, all day, [4] . ► Thirst. ► Thirst and diminished appetite, [2] . ► Eructation and Hiccough. ► Eructations after eating, [2] . [80.] ► Frequent eructations, [16] . ► Sour eructations, [2] . ► Foul eructations, [2] . ► Eructations tasting of the ingesta, [2] . ► Hiccough after eating, [2] . ► Nausea and Vomiting. ► Nausea, [16] . ► Nausea, in the morning, [2] . ► Nausea, vomiting, and weakness of the stomach, [13] . ► Excessive nausea, [16] . ► Excessive nausea, with diarrha, [19] . ► Stomach. [90.] ► Gastro-enteritis, [19] . ► Disorders of the stomach, during menstruation, [2] . ► Sort of faintness at the stomach, without any real appetite, [2] . ► Cardialgia and vomiting, [5] . ► Burning pain in the stomach, [2] . ► The whole epigastric region is tensive and painful to contact, [2] . ► Spasm of the stomach, [2] . ► Pressure at the pit of the stomach, even before breakfast, [2] . ► Stitches in the stomach, at intervals, as if in paroxysms, [2] . ► Beating at the pit of the stomach, followed by palpitation of the heart, and obnubilation on leaving the table, after eating, [2] . ABDOMEN. ► Hypochondria. [100.] ► Stitchings in the hypochondria, [2] . ► Pressive, at times pulsating, pain in the hepatic region, [2] . ► General Abdomen. ► Rumbling with flatulence in the intestines, and a sensation as if colic would come on, [16] . ► Noisy borborygmi, [2] . ► Emission of extremely fetid flatulence, in the morning, in bed, and after rising, [2] . ► Griping, [16] . ► Violent grips in the abdomen, [19] . ► Pinching in the abdomen, [2] . ► Violent cutting pains, followed by two diarrhic stools, immediately after taking a cup of café au lait (as usual), [2] . ► Colic and looseness , [3] . [110.] ► Painful colic, [16] . ► Hypogastrium and Iliac Regions. ► Swelling of the lower abdomen, [2] . ► Pressure in the lower abdomen, as from a stone, [2] . ► Beatings in the lower abdomen, [2] . ► Swelling (not very large) of the inguinal ganglia, [2] . ► Swelling and sensitiveness of the inguinal glands, [21] . ► The inguinal glands are painful to contact, [2] . RECTUM AND ANUS. ► Rectum. ► Spasms of the rectum, [2] . ► Pressure in the rectum, causing a constant urging to stool, [2] . ► Stitches in the rectum, [2] , [21] . ► Anus. [120.] ► Bleeding piles, [2] . ► Constant oozing from the anus, of a serous or even purulent liquid, [2] . ► Burning itching at the anus, [2] . STOOL. ► Diarrha. ► Diarrha, in the morning, [2] . ► A diarrha accompanied the exudation from the skin, [12] . ► Diarrha of whitish fæces, especially in the morning, with coldness and drawing tearing in the abdomen, which obliged him to bend double, before and after an evacuation, [1] . ► Diarrha, alternating with obstinate constipation, [21] . ► Violent diarrha in one night (fifteen evacuations in ten hours), with spasms at the stomach, coldness of the extremities and cramps in the calves, [2] . ► Frightful diarrha, [13] . ► Frequent excretion of fæces, which are at first shaped, but afterwards pasty, and at last mucous and mixed with blood, [15] . [130.] ► Several soft stools every day, followed by general prostration, [2] . ► Stools thin, two or three a day, without pain and without urging; there seems to be an increase of secretion of mucus in the rectum, [22] . ► Copious stools, [3] . ► Whitish, fecal stools of a sour smell, with discharge of ascarides, [2] . ► Stools like sheeps' dung, [2] . ► Stools at first dry and formed, and ending in diarrha, [2] . ► Two liquid stools, [16] . ► Involuntary diarrhic stool, [1] . ► Constipation. ► Insufficient stools, [2] . ► No stool for five days, [2] . URINARY ORGANS. ► Bladder. [140.] ► Dull pain in the bladder, [2] . ► *Burning in the neck of the bladder and urethra , [21] . ► Pressure on the bladder, [2] . ► Pressure on the bladder, with frequent ineffectual urging to urinate, and passage of urine drop by drop, [21] . ► Urethra. ► Inflammation of the urinary passages and adjacent parts. It has also been known to cause inflammation of the urethra, retention of urine, phlegmasia of the bladder, of the prostate, the anus, and the rectum. It is interesting to see this medicine prescribed by some physicians for almost the same complaints which others have remarked that it causes , [9] . ► Inflammation and swelling of the urethra, with pains along the whole penis, [21] . ► Discharge of mucus from the urethra, [21] . ► Mucous discharge from the urethra, [2] . ► Discharge of a milky, acrid, corrosive character, with painful micturition, [21] . ► Profuse milky discharge for three days, which stops of itself, although the use of the drug is continued, [2] . [150.] ► The urethral discharge was inversely as that from the skin, [12] . ► The urethra discharge ceased, but returned in four or five days, with the decline of the eruption, [12] . ► Purulent blennorrhagia, [1] . ► Burning in the urethra, when urinating, [1] . ► Burning and itching in the urethra and vagina, [21] . ► Drawings in the urethra, [22] . ► Pain at the orifice of the urethra, as if wounded, [1] . ► Pulsating pain in the urethra, when not urinating; orifice of the urethra gaping, tumid, inflamed, [1] . ► Tickling in the orifice of the urethra, [21] . ► Itching and biting in the urethra, before and after urinating, [16] . [160.] ► Frequent urging to urinate, immediately after urinating, [22] . ► Frequent unsuccessful urging to urinate, [2] . ► Constant urging to urinate, [1] . ► Micturition. ► Frequent emission of urine, mostly in small quantities, [15] . ► Frequent micturition during the night; urine copious and lighter than formerly, [22] . ► Copious secretion of urine smelling of the balsam, [21] . ► Urine more copious and frequent than usual, [16] . ► The urine is more abundant and more acid, and has a very strong smell of violets, [3] . ► Diuresis, [19] . ► Bloody micturition, [21] . [170.] ► Bloody urine, strangury, and ischuria, [17] . ► Painful urination, [21] . ► The urine is discharged drop by drop, [1] . ► Retention of the urine, [21] . ► Urine. ► The urine had a very strong odor of Copaiba, frothed very much, was dark-yellow, and had an acid reaction, [22] . ► Bitter taste to the urine, [17] . ► Albuminuria, lasting several days, [19] . SEXUAL ORGANS. ► Male. ► Superficial excoriation on the glans and on the prepuce, [2] . ► Excessive erections, with lascivious thoughts, and constant excitement of sexual desire, [21] . ► Violent erections on the following days (night and morning), with or without lascivious thoughts, [2] . [180.] ► Absence of erections the first days, [2] . ► Long-continued tickling in the glans penis, lasting several hours, [22] . ► Unpleasant itching at the tip of the glans, [2] . ► Redness and slight oozing between the scrotum and the thigh, [2] . ► Swelling of one of the testicles (left), which is very sensitive to touch, [2] . ► Dull, heavy pain in the testicles, [2] . ► Pressive drawing in the testicles, [21] . ► Constant and permanent sexual excitement (during the whole time of the proving), [2] . ► Violent desire for an embrace, with absence of erection, [2] . ► Female. ► Throbbing and pain in the ovarian region, [21] . [190.] ► Beating in the right ovarian region, when standing, [2] . ► Spasms of the uterus, [2] . ► Drawings in the uterus, orifice of the urethra, and in the vagina, [21] . ► Dragging on the uterus and bladder, [21] . ► Pulling pains in the suspensory ligaments of the uterus, [2] . ► Constant pressure on the uterus, as if prolapsus would set in, [2] . ► Burning red spots in the vulva, [2] . ► Itching of the vulva , [2] . ► Deep stitches in the vagina and neck of the womb, [1] . ► Leucorrha after the menses, [2] . [200.] ► A leucorrha of long standing disappears during the use of the drug, [2] . ► Milky leucorrha, [2] . ► Acrid leucorrha, causing excoriation of the vulva, [2] . ► The menses are too early by three days, [2] . ► The menses are too early by seven days, [2] . ► The menses are too early by ten days, [2] . ► The menses appear on the regular day, but are very pale, and much less abundant than usual, [2] . ► The menses reappear after they had ceased for several days, [2] . ► Metrorrhagia, [1] . RESPIRATORY ORGANS. ► Larynx, Trachea, and Bronchi. ► Dryness and roughness in the larynx, [2] . [210.] ► During the menses, tightness at the larynx, hoarseness in the morning; dry cough in the morning, [2] . ► Excoriating pain in the larynx, [2] . ► Voice. ► Hoarseness, especially in the morning, with excoriating pain in the larynx, when talking, [2] . ► The voice, though not much altered, loses its compass during the whole time of the proving; the lower notes are unchanged, but the higher notes cause an excoriating pain, which finally makes their rendering impossible, [2] . ► Cough and Expectoration. ► Cough excited by tickling in the larynx, trachea and bronchi, [2] . ► Dry cough, morning and evening, [2] . ► Cough with profuse whitish expectoration, sometimes saltish, sometimes flat and nauseous, [2] . ► Rough cough, with difficult expectoration of greenish mucus, [2] . ► Respiration. ► Foul breath in the morning, [2] . ► Slow respiration, [15] . CHEST. [220.] ► Burning in the chest, [2] . ► Sense of fulness at the chest, which frequently obliges one to sigh, [2] . ► Oppression of the chest, with labored breathing, while working in a stooping position, as when digging, [2] . ► Front. ► Pressure on the sternum, [2] . ► Sides. ► Stitches in the right side of the chest, [2] . HEART AND PULSE. ► Frequent and very feeble pulsation of the heart, [15] . ► Palpitation of the heart, during work, [2] . NECK AND BACK. ► Neck. ► Rheumatic pain in the nape of the neck and left side of the neck, [2] . ► Back. ► Stiffness in the back, which ceases when walking, [2] . ► Dorsal. ► Pullings in both shoulder-blades, [2] . [230.] ► Stitches in the left shoulder-blade, [2] . ► Stitches between the shoulders, cutting short the respiration, [2] . ► Burning pain in the dorsal spine, [2] . ► Lumbar. ► Lumbar pains, [19] . ► Pains dull, and as if spasmodic, in the lumbar region, [2] . EXTREMITIES IN GENERAL. ► Trembling of the limbs, [2] . ► Twitchings of the limbs, during rest, [2] . SUPERIOR EXTREMITIES. ► Numbness of the arm on which one is lying, at night, [2] . ► Shoulder. ► Pain of dislocation in the right shoulder, [2] . ► Acute pain in the left shoulder, [2] . [240.] ► Acute stitches at the left axilla, penetrating into the chest, [2] . ► The axillary glands are sensitive to contact, [2] . ► Elbow. ► Violent pressure in the right elbow joint, in the evening; during the previous night it had been in the other shoulder, [22] . ► Forearm. ► Pullings in the forearms, [2] . ► Wrist. ► Violent pain in the left wrist, [22] . ► Hand. ► Trembling in the hands, [2] . ► Fingers. ► Stiffness in the fingers, [2] . INFERIOR EXTREMITIES. ► Numbness of the limbs, when sitting, [2] . ► Intolerable uneasiness in the lower limbs, [2] . ► Hip. ► Rheumatic pains (as if caused by great fatigue) in the left hip and knee, with spasms of the uterus, during menstruation, [2] . [250.] ► Cramplike pain in both hips, worse in the right, [2] . ► Bruised pain in the right hip, when lying on it, [2] . ► Bruised pain in the right hip, when walking and when touching it, [2] . ► Thigh. ► Before the gripings, constrictive pain in the hollow of the femoral bone, [1] . ► Knee. ► Cracking in the knees, when stretching the legs, [2] . ► Dull pain in the knees, [2] . ► Pressure in the left knee, while walking, [22] . ► Ankle. ► Violent pain in the right ankle, while walking and during rest, [22] . ► Foot. ► Swelling of both feet, [2] . ► Swelling of the left foot, [2] . [260.] ► Pain, as if sprained, in the feet, very troublesome when first commencing to walk, and which disappears while walking, [2] . ► During the cold stage, pain on the dorsum of the foot, during motion, [1] . GENERALITIES. ► Objective. ► Spasmodic paroxysms and other hysterical symptoms, [2] . ► Languor, with anxious sadness, [2] . ► Muscular weakness increases, [15] . ► Subjective. ► Excessive sensitiveness of the whole nervous system, so that noise makes him start, and angers him, [2] . ► Extreme sensitiveness to cold, dampness, [2] . ► In most cases a diminished sensibility, [15] . ► Pulling in all the muscles, especially in the evening and at night, [2] . ► Pulsations here and there, [2] . SKIN. ► Objective. ► Eruptions, Dry. [270.] ► Eruption on the skin, consisting of isolated, sharply defined, somewhat elevated bright-red spots, with elevations similar to the sting of a wasp, confluent on the ears and backs of the hands; after a few days they became brownish-yellow, like the so-called liver-spots; there was no desquamation except on the ears, which was a fine mealy desquamation; even after four weeks burning spots could be seen, [20] . ► Eruption resembling roseola, but the spots are more elevated and larger, confluent on certain portions of the chest and arms, and look something like petechiæ; the eruption disappears at the end of four days, without perceptible desquamation, [11] . ► Uneasiness during two days, followed by a very hard chill, which is succeeded by heat and an eruption of very circumscribed lenticular patches; this eruption gives rise to intense itching and pricking of the skin; it is like measles, but is without any catarrhal symptoms. On the third day the fever subsides, and the eruption grows paler. On the seventh day the skin has a mottled appearance. The eruption does not wholly disappear for five days; no desquamation , [7] . ► Eruption of a number of red blotches all over the body, but especially on the face, [3] . ► Red miliary eruption, sometimes discrete, sometimes confluent, preceded by headache, vertigo, and loathing, [5] . ► In a few days a general copaival erythema declared itself, with the usual character of that eruption, [12] . ► Copaiba produces a rash well described by Judd in his work; a rosy erythema of "pumiceous" aspect, as though the skin had been bitten by insects, [11] . ► Eruption like scarlet fever or nettlerash over the whole body; this eruption appeared first in the face, especially on the forehead, afterwards on the backs of the hands, then on other portions of the body, with severe burning in the skin, [20] . ► *Nettlerash (several authors). ► Large red blotches all over the body, with constipation and some fever , [6] . [280.] ► Elevated eruption, confluent on the hands and feet, scarlet, or like elevated measles, [13] . ► Small furfuraceous tetter on the concha of the left ear, with burning pain only when touched, [2] . ► Urticaria, at first on the face, especially the forehead, then on the back of the hands, and finally in other parts. Burning of the skin, slight sore throat (without swelling of the pharynx or tonsils), and sensation as if a violent perspiration would break out, as it does afterwards. No fever or thirst; red, distinct raised spots, like bug-bites, running together only on the ears and the backs of the hands. On the sixth day the redness disappears, and the spots assume a brown color, without desquamating, except on the ears, [8] . ► Small, red, slightly itching pimples on the upper part of the forehead, on hairy scalp, above the ears and on the occiput, [2] . ► Red itching spot on the left arm, [2] . ► Red slightly itching spots at the sternal region and the right axilla, [2] . ► Yellowish spots of the size of a dime, of irregular shapes, and slightly itching, on the right cheek, morning and evening, [2] . ► Eruptions, Moist. ► The eruption which now appeared was a modified pemphigus, and was generalized over the whole body, commencing at the flexures of the joints. Where the skin was thin the bullæ aborted, but where it was thicker they were regularly raised and more or less lasting. The discharge from the patches deprived of their epidermis was excessive, of an offensive odor, and very viscid, [12] . ► After six weeks of emollient treatment the pemphigus disappeared in a general desquamation, [12] . ► Red humid tetter (after eight days) on the upper lip, which is swollen and painful when touched, [2] . [290.] ► Small white miliary vesicles on a red base on the left wing and tip of the nose, [2] . ► Redness and moist acridity on the scrotum and between it and the thighs, [21] . ► Eruptions, Pustular. ► Tormenting eruptions only between the fingers and on both forearms as far as the bend of the elbow; pustules like the itch, with red areolæ, containing a red watery fluid, with itching pain, [18] . ► Mild eruptive fever, with inconsiderable general heat and moderated headache. An eruption quite like that of distinct variola comes out on the third day; it is purple-hued and not very well marked. In twelve hours it spreads, sinks down, and each of its pustules becoming depressed, soon forms only a petechial blotch, which then proceeds to enlarge itself, to be confounded with adjacent blotches, and thus to become confluent. It gradually fades away, and on the fifth day disappears, [10] . ► Boil on the pubis, [2] . ► Subjective. ► Burning in the skin of the scrotum, [2] . ► Itching of the cheeks, here and there, in the eyebrows and on the chin, [2] . SLEEP AND DREAMS. ► Sleepiness. ► *Drowsiness in the daytime , [2] . ► Sleeplessness. ► Sleeplessness and great restlessness all night, after an embrace, [2] . ► *Restless sleep at night , [2] . [300.] ► Frequent waking, [2] . ► Wakes early and is unable to fall asleep again, [2] . ► Dreams. ► Frightful or lascivious dreams, [2] . FEVER. ► Chilliness. ► Fever for several days in succession; in the morning coldness, with shudderings; in the afternoon general heat and thirst, [1] . ► Coldness of the feet, knees, and thighs at night, [2] . ► Icy coldness of the feet, from morning till noon, [2] . ► Cold hands, [2] . ► Cold feet and knees during the menses, [2] . ► Sweat. ► Sweat, [16] . ► Sensation as though copious sweat would break out, which afterwards took place in bed, [20] . [310.] ► Profuse and inodorous sweat in the morning, [2] . ► Sour-smelling sweat at night, [2] . ► The whole of the Copaiba seems to have been absorbed and to have exercised its influence upon the skin, by which it is always more or less eliminated, giving its odor to the perspiration, [12] . CONDITIONS. ► Aggravation. ► ( Morning ), Soon after waking, sadness; during menses, sadness, etc.; when washing face with cold water, stitches in temples; stitches in occipital protuberance; contraction of orbicularis muscle; stoppage of nose; nausea; in bed, and after rising, emission of flatulence; diarrha , etc.; hoarseness, etc.; foul breath; coldness, etc. ► ( Forenoon ), When riding on horseback, vertigo; headache; coldness of feet. ► ( Afternoon ), Sadness, etc.; general heat, etc. ► ( Evening ), When going to bed, hungry; pressure in elbow-joint; pulling in all muscles. ► ( Night ), Pain in temple; frequent micturition; pain in hips; pulling in all muscles; coldness of feet, etc. ► ( After café au lait ), Immediately, cutting pains, etc. ► ( During and after eating ), Rush of blood to head, etc. ► ( During menses ), Cold feet, etc. ► ( Pressing part against pillow ), Pain in temple. ► ( During rest ), Twitching of limbs. ► ( When sitting ), Numbness of legs. ► ( When standing ), Beating in ovarian region. ► ( Standing upright and motionless ), Vertigo. ► ( Stooping while working ), Oppression of chest, etc. ► ( After urinating ), Itching, etc.; in urethra. ► ( While walking ), Pressure in knee. ► ( When commencing to walk ), Pain in feet. ► ( During work ), Palpitation of heart. ► Amelioration. ► ( Pressure ), Pain in temporal region. ► ( During a walk ), Sadness; reverberation of sounds in head; stiffness in back. ► ( Continuing to walk ), Pain in feet. SUPPLEMENT: COPAIVA. Authorities. 23 , Dr. A. Kennedy, Month. Hom. Rev., vol. xx, p. 479, 1876, J. McK., æt. twenty-two years, took the oil for gonorrha; 24 , F. Taylor, M.D., Guy's Hosp. Rep., 1876, p. 1, Weickart's experiments, took 1 1/2 ounce of the Mistura Copaibæ Resinæ , containing about 20 grains, three hours after a full meal; 25 , ibid., took 3 ounces of mixture two hours after a full meal. ► The urine passed after forty-five minutes gave no precipitate nor turbidity with Nitric acid, but some passed after one hour and twenty minutes became opalescent on the addition of either Nitric or Acetic acid. The same reaction occurred with that passed two hours after, but not with the next instalment, passed nine hours after taking the drug, [24] . ► The urine passed after twenty, forty, and sixty minutes gave no reaction, but a turbidity was produced in that passed one hour and twenty-five minutes after the dose, and the precipitate was abundant in that discharged fifty minutes later. Twelve hours after taking the medicine the urine became only faintly opalescent on the addition of Nitric acid, and it was not further tested, [25] . ► Three weeks ago contracted a first gonorrha, for which he used injections of Sulphate of Zinc. These arrested the discharge for a time, but it returned, and he began to take the oil of Copaiba. On may 19th he took two tablespoonfuls of the pure oil, besides having taken one tablespoonful two or three days previously. After each dose he was sick and vomited a little. On the 20th the discharge stopped; he had a rigor, and noticed his face swollen and red. Went to business without taking food. On the 21st he had great thirst, nausea, vomiting, complete anorexia, restlessness, with increase of swelling and redness of face. The arms and legs, and finally the body, became red and swollen. When seen, May 22d, I found the whole body dematous, face dusky yellowish-red color; dema, worst about eyelids, which could not be opened; some sticky discharge at margins of eyelids; surface of skin of face and neck raised rather like measles. This is more observable where the raised eruption terminates, next the hair and at lower parts of neck: Over the body is a darkly-reddish smooth eruption, studded with innumerable points of a deeper color scattered over the

Clinical: Acne; Anus, itching of; Bladder, irritable; Blenorrhagia; Bronchitis; Catarrh; Cough; Cystitis; Dysentery; Fistula; Gastritis; Gonorrhœa; Hæmorrhoids; Measles; Nose-bleed; Prostatitis; Pruritus; Urethritis; Urticaria; Vulva, inflammation of

Dry, painful cough, with dryness in the larynx. Profuse expectoration of a greenish-grey purulent mucus, of a disgusting smell. Spitting of blood. Pain and swelling of the knees and malleoli. Burning pain (skin, abdomen, urethra, prostate gland, chest). Heaviness and pressure (abdomen, chest, perineum).

Greatly excited during night. θ Urticaria. General malaise. θ Nettlerash. Weariness ; debility, unable to sit up. θ Bronchial and vesical catarrh. Involuntary trembling and movements of head, trunk and extremities. Hemiplegia after disappearance of a measle-like eruption which had covered whole body. Frequent : mucous diarrhœa ; calls to urinate. Spasmodic : pains in bladder, every morning at same hour. Occasional : stitches in left side of chest. Long-continued : weakness of sexual organs. During two days : uneasiness, followed by chill, heat and eruption, which subside on third day. Constant : ineffectual desire to urinate ; excitement of sexual desire ; irritation in larynx, exciting cough. Lasting several days : epistaxis of small boys. Chronic : nasal catarrh ; inflammation of throat ; diarrhœa and dysentery ; hemorrhoidal tumors ; cough with fistula in ano ; vesical catarrh ; cystitis ; gleet ; bronchorrhœa ; pulmonary catarrh. Right : pains from scrotum and anus extend down to side of foot. Left : mucous rales in lung ; occasional stitches in side of chest ; dulness under clavicle and want of normal resonance on whole side. As if abdomen would burst ; as if respiratory passage was filled with mucus. Pain : in mouth, throat and joints ; in stomach and limbs ; in bladder every morning ; in neck of bladder on attempting to urinate ; in prostate gland on urinating ; under fourchette when urine reaches glans penis ; in knees ; in scrotum and anus, extending down right side of foot, with severe cramps ; in dorsa of feet ; in ovaries. Stitches : in rectum ; in left side of chest. Tearing : colicky pains in abdomen. Drawing-tearing : colic during stool ; in abdomen ; in thighs. Drawing : in bones of thighs ; in uterus, mouth of bladder and vagina. Aching-drawing : in testicles. Itching-drawing : in testicles. Burning : in abdomen and umbilical region ; intolerable, at anus ; in neck of bladder and urethra ; in urethra ; in region of prostate gland ; in vagina ; red spots in vulva ; in palms of hands and soles of feet ; in chest. Forcing down : in bladder and uterine region. Throbbing : in ovaries. Pulsative : pain throughout penis. Twitching : in skin, urethra. Biting : in urethra ; of skin. Pricking : in skin. Excoriating pain : in larynx. Smarting : of urethra. Soreness : of urethra ; of meatus urinarius ; of chest. Rawness : in chest. Roughness : in larynx. Wandering uterine pains. Pressure : in stomach ; in abdomen and umbilical region ; on bladder ; in uterus ; on sternum ; in chest. Trembling : in head. Fulness : after eating. Oppression : of chest. Weight : in perineum. Anxiety : in chest. Uneasiness : in stomach. Tickling : at orifice of urethra. Dryness : of tongue ; in region of prostate gland, and in urethra ; in larynx. Warmth : in vagina. Itching : of eyes ; in urethra ; of measle-like eruption ; in vagina ; of vulva ; of skin ; of nettlerash. Blennorrhagia or profuse discharges from various mucous surfaces ; chronic catarrh. Inflammatory affections of intestinal canal, genital and urinary systems. Vesical and urethral catarrh of both sexes. Emaciation. θ Bronchial and vesical catarrh. Post-scarlatinal dropsy. Dropsy from liver and heart. Ascites, general anasarca from renal disease, especially albuminuria after scarlatina. Gonorrhœal rheumatism. TOUCH. PASSIVE MOTION. INJURIES. [45] [Cop.]. Touch : urticaria, skin disagreeable. Girl, æt. 4, scrofulous ; dysuria. Boy, æt. 12 ; urticaria. Mr. R., æt. 12 ; urticaria. Miss G., æt. 15 ; lymphatic-sanguine temperament ; caught severe cold ; bronchial and vesical catarrh. Miss B., æt. 24 ; urticaria and gastric disturbances. MS., æt. 25, teacher, delicate health, and frequently suffering from throat affections, neuralgic toothache, gastralgia, with constipation ; all her ailments are more on r. side ; urticaria. Young German girl ; gonorrhœa. A mason, suffering a year with urinary difficulties arising from catching cold and indulgence in beer and spirits. Man, æt. 48, with tendency to hemorrhoids, had gonorrhœa several times, in consequence of which he suffered from strictures and dysuria, he was relieved by caustics, nevertheless great irritability or urethra remained ; dysuria. Man, æt. 50, suffering ten years from fistula in ano conjoined with a chronic cough ; had an attack of gonorrhœa, for which large doses of balsam copaiva were given, which resulted in curing the fistula and cough. Mrs. B., æt. 62 ; nettlerash.

keynotes.text: Copaiva is an old-world remedy for gonorrhœa, and it has also been used for other blenorrhagic conditions. It has been proved; and observations have been made on patients who have overdosed themselves with the drug. The chief incidence of the drug's action is on the genito-urinary organs, and rectum. It is suited to the irritable bladder of old women. Irritation accompanying thickening of bladder. Burning at neck of bladder and in urethra; discharge milky, corrosive; meatus tumid and inflamed. It affects the skin and mucous membranes generally. In chronic bronchitis it is useful when there is profuse, greenish-grey, disgusting-smelling sputa. A. P. Bowie (Med. Adv., xx. 14) has verified this indication: "In old persons (men, I should say) who from cold or other causes cannot urinate; or where there is frequent desire and only a few drops pass with much straining─frequently with a mucous discharge from the bowels, with much rumbling and rolling in the bowels." He gave 5-drop doses of the first dilution. He commends also an ointment of gtt. x of the balsam to one ounce of vaseline, "to relieve the burning and itching at the anus caused by piles."

This remedy acts strongly upon mucous membranes. It has, like many other remedies, been so abused by the old school that it has fallen into disrepute, and the tendency even with our own school under such circumstances is to underrate its virtues or to fail to investigate as they ought to. It is, however, an excellent remedy in the form of chronic bronchial catarrh, with profuse expectoration of greenish or gray purulent matter. (Stannum, Lycopodium, Sulphur, Phosphorus, etc.) Among remedies not yet well understood we have:
Copaiva, profuse greenish-gray, disgusting smelling sputa.
Illicium anisatum, pus, with pain at third cartilage, right or left.
Pix liquida, purulent sputa, pain at left third costal cartilage.
Myosotis, copious sputa, emaciation, night sweats.
Balsam Peru. catarrhal phthisis, copious purulent expectoration.
Yerba santa, accumulation of mucus causing asthmatic breathing.
I mention these remedies in order to call attention to them for trial in case we cannot find the curative among the better proven ones. All the old remedies had to have their beginnings.

Copaiva is a valuable remedy in gonorrhœa. There is great irritation in the urethra and at the neck of the bladder. It may be indicated in the beginning, when the discharge is thin or milky, and later, especially when the disease has extended to the bladder, with discharge of a large amount of viscid mucus or blood and mucus in the urine. Although not so violent in its action on the urinary tract as Cantharis, it stands very close to it.

_raw: Antidoted by: Bell., Calc., Merc., Sulph. Compare: Cannab., Canth., Cubeb., Eriger., Kali bro., Kali i., Sep., Senec. It has less violent action than Canth. Teste says he is unable to find any difference between the therapeutic properties of Copaiv. and Sep. Both are antidoted by the same drug: Merc. cor. in the male, and Merc. sol. in the female, according to Teste, neutralise the action of Copaiv. almost instantaneously.
Antidoted by : Bellad., Calcar., Mercur., Sulphur
Compatible : After Acon., when acute symptoms of gonorrhœa have been subdued
Compare : Cannab., Canthar., Cubeba, Erig., Kali brom., Pulsat., Kali iod., Sepia., Senec. grac., all of which cause analogous symptoms of mucous membrane, especially of urinary organs

mental_emotional.text: Depression with anxious sadness. Excessive sensitiveness of whole nervous system; least noise causes starting and anger. A young girl weeps on hearing a piano. Uneasiness on account of one's health. Misanthropy. Deficient memory. Loathing of life, and at the same time fear of death.
General sense of uneasiness ; depression. Delirium. θ Urticaria. Delirium, intense headache, drowsiness, embarrassed speech. θ Urticaria. Vertigo.

head.text: Heaviness of head, esp. of occipital region, which is instinctively pressed against collar of coat, which relieves. Pulsative, deep stitches in occiput. Dull pain in occiput. Every step jars the head. Hemicrania (l.) with burning pains, sense of coldness in part, weeping and constant moaning (in young man of twenty-two, disposed to be hypochondriacal. It was so violent that Teste had to give Merc. to relieve him). Pressure at forehead. Headache > pressing head back against collar of coat; gentle pressure with hand evening and night; intolerable when leaning on pillow; washing face with cool water in morning (sudden stitches in both temples). Falling out of the hair. Sensitiveness of hairy scalp.
Violent headache. θ Urticaria. Headache as from congestion, with heat in face and sensation of trembling in head ; delirium. Headache and general malaise, with chills. θ Nettlerash.

eyes.text: Formication in canthi of eyes in evening. Black points hovering before eyes. On alternately closing eyes objects look much paler to l. eye than to r.
Inflammation and itching of eyes ; dim-sightedness.

ears.text: Excessive sensitiveness of hearing, esp. to sharp sounds.

nose.text: Epistaxis of small boys; also after wounds. Profuse yellow and green, thick, fetid discharge from nasal passages running down throat at night.

Severe spontaneous epistaxis of small boys, lasting several days. Epistaxis ; hemorrhages after wounds. (OBS :) Profuse, yellow and green, thick, fetid discharge from nasal passages of several years' duration ; at night the discharge runs down posterior nares, causing choking and other disagreeable symptoms. θ Chronic nasal catarrh.

face.text: Face pale and sickly-looking. Acne. Urticaria. Red humid tetter on upper lip, which is swollen and painful when touched.

Flushed face, interspersed with rose-colored spots. θ Urticaria. Red face and urticaria over whole body ; face, eyelids, lips, ears and hands swollen. Acne which had for a long time disfigured face. θ Urinary troubles.

mouth_throat.text: Teeth set on edge. Sense of coldness in teeth. Foul breath in morning. Tongue covered with whitish coating, greenish at base. Tenacious phlegm in mouth and throat, which is constantly reproduced. Chronic catarrh of throat. Sensation of foreign body in pharynx. Swelling of both tonsils ( r.).

Speech embarrassed, cannot talk well. θ Urticaria. Tongue coated white. θ Bronchial and vesical catarrh. Pain in mouth, throat and joints. Slight but painful swelling of lips and mouth. Dryness of tongue, with inflammation of throat and mouth. Increased flow of saliva. Chronic inflammation of throat. Marked inflammation of throat with great swelling of submaxillary glands and threatened suffocation.

stomach_abdomen.text: Thirst and diminished appetite. Bitter taste. Hungry in evening on going to bed. Every article of food seems too salt. Rush of blood to head and face when eating or after a meal. Spitting up of ingesta with large quantities of mucus. Inclination to vomit. Distension and fulness after eating; gastric troubles during menstruation or following urticaria. Pressing pain in region of spleen, becomes pulsative from time to time. Tearings in the abdomen preceded by pullings in the bones of the thighs. Sensation of burning in the abdomen. Borborygmi and movements in the intestines.

APPETITE, THIRST. DESIRES, AVERSIONS. [14] [Cop.]. Loss of appetite. θ Bronchial and vesical catarrh. θ Urticaria. Great thirst. θ Urticaria. Eructations, excessive nausea ; nausea in throat ; gagging and vomiting. Great nausea with severe colic, somewhat > by sweat. Spitting up of ingesta, with large quantities of mucus ; sometimes vomiting of mucus. θ Gastric catarrh. Heartburn ; pressure and uneasiness in stomach. Pain in stomach and limbs. Distension and fulness after eating ; spitting up of ingesta, with large quantities of mucus ; stools coated with mucus ; frequent mucous diarrhœa. θ Gastric catarrh. Gastric troubles during menstruation, or following urticaria. Borborygmus ; rumbling in abdomen so loud that it is heard by others ; abdomen distended as if it would burst. Pressure and burning in abdomen in umbilical region. Severe colic with great nausea, somewhat > by sweat. Colic, with flatulency, indigestion and looseness. Colic, with watery diarrhœa ; colicky tearing pains in abdomen, preceded by drawing pains in bones of thighs. Hypogastrium sensitive. θ Dysentery. Painful swelling of inguinal glands.

stool_rectum.text: White, loose evacuations, chiefly in the morning, with cold and drawing tearings in the abdomen, which force the patient to bend double. Involuntary evacuations. Stools like sheep's dung. Insufficient stools. Fluent piles. Bloody stools. Stools with tenesmus. Stitches; spasms in rectum. Intolerable burning at the anus. Burning, itching at anus.

Diarrhœa, with loss of appetite ; nausea and vomiting ; copious, involuntary, watery stools ; < in morning ; colic, > by bending double. White, diarrheic stools, generally in morning, with chilliness and colic, obliging one to bend double. Greenish passages, mixed mucous flocculi. θ Bronchial and vesical catarrh. White, diarrheic, mucous stools in morning, mucus is not tenacious or stringy, but comes away in masses. θ Intestinal catarrh. Stools often coated with mucus ; frequent attacks of mucous diarrhœa. θ Gastric catarrh. Debilitating diarrhœa, with tenesmus. Chronic diarrhœa and dysentery, with or without fever. Diarrhœa alternating with obstinate constipation. Constipation, with large red blotches over whole body, and fever. Stools : white, fecal ; bloody ; watery ; copious ; involuntary. During stool : chilliness, tenesmus, drawing, tearing colic. Stitches in rectum and intolerable burning at anus. Hemorrhoidal tumors lasting for years disappeared under its use, in a case of gonorrhœa. (OBS :) Fistula in ano of ten years standing, with a chronic cough. Sensation of weight in perineum. Expulsion of several yards of tapeworm.

urinary.text: Constant, ineffectual desire to urinate; contraction of the urethra; emission of urine in drops. Itching, soreness, and sensation of scalding in the urethra, before and after the emission of urine. Inflammation of the urinary organs; swelling, dilatation, and inflammation of the orifice of the urethra. Pain as from excoriation in the orifice of the urethra, which remains wide open, with throbbing pain in the penis generally. Hæmaturia. Urine foaming; greenish-turbid, smelling like violets. Yellow, purulent gonorrhœa.

Inflammation of kidneys. θ Bright's disease. Chronic vesical catarrh ; excessive irritation of bladder. Spasmodic pains in bladder every morning, at nearly same hour. θ Bronchial and vesical catarrh. Frequent calls to urinate, with violent pains in neck of bladder on attempting it, occasional retention of urine ; after catching cold. Pressure on bladder, with fruitless urinary tenesmus and passage of urine in drops. Tenesmus of bladder and urethritis. Irritation of urethra and neck of bladder, occurring in old women. Burning in neck of bladder and urethra. Induration of prostate gland, and great pain in it on urinating. Inflammation of urinary organs ; swelling, dilatation and tickling at orifice of urethra, with pulsative pain throughout penis. θ Gonorrhœa. Urinary difficulties with hæmaturia. Copious secretion or retention of urine. Constant ineffectual desire to urinate ; contraction of urethra ; emission of urine in drops. Great dysuria. Urine can only be discharged after great effort ; stream thinner than usual, causing great pain under fourchette, when reaching glans penis. θ Dysuria after gonorrhœa. Itching, biting and burning in urethra, both before and after urination. Urine emitted in drops. θ Induration of prostate. Urine : foaming ; greenish ; turbid ; with odor of violets. Urine scant and full of sediment, with burning in urethra after micturition. θ Urticaria. Urine high colored and thick, depositing abundant reddish sediment. θ Bronchial and vesical catarrh. Dark red, scanty urine, with brick-dust sediment. θ Urticaria. Urine hot and scalding, red, depositing thick, mucous sediment. θ Dysentery. Evacuations of thick, brown, tenacious mucus, tinged with blood ; tormina and tenesmus ; sensitive hypogastrium. θ Dysentery. Large amount of viscid mucus, some blood and detached portions of mucous tissue in urine. θ Bronchial and vesical catarrh. Hæmaturia in women. Frequent copious discharges of urine, with diabetes.

male.text: Burning and sensation of dryness in the region of the prostate gland; induration of the prostate gland. Swelling of testes. Swelling and induration of the testes.

Violent emotions, with lascivious thoughts, constant excitement of sexual desire. Long-continued weakness of sexual organs with absence of sexual desire, and irritation of the parts. Chronic cystitis. Induration of prostate with no increase in size, or slight augmentation with extreme hardness. Burning and sensation of dryness in region of prostate gland, and in urethra. Soreness, smarting, itching and swelling of urethra ; purulent discharge ; urine smells of violets ; eruption like measles or urticaria with great itching. Biting pain, burning and itching in urethra before and after urination ; meatus swollen and inflamed, with soreness and discharge of pus. θ Gonorrhœa. Swelling and dilatation of orifice of urethra, with pulsative pains throughout penis. Gonorrhœa in the early stage, no strangury. Gonorrhœa : mild cases, or in first part of second stage, when discharge is moderate ; much urinary irritation, but no particular difficulty. Yellow, purulent, or mucous discharge from urethra. Chronic gleet with muco-purulent discharge. Swelling and induration of testicles. Aching-drawing in testicles. Redness and acrid humor on scrotum and between it and thighs, swelling and tenderness of inguinal glands. Gonorrhœal rheumatism.

female.text: Burning and itching of urethra; itching of vulva; burning red spots in vulva. Milky, acid, excoriating discharge with painful menstruation. Profuse gonorrhœal discharge. Metrorrhagia.

Throbbing and pains in ovaries. Forcing down in bladder and uterine region ; drawing in uterus, neck of bladder and vagina ; burning and itching in urethra and vagina. Wandering uterine pains. Uterine hemorrhages. Discharge of bloody and thick, purulent mucus from uterus with pressure. Leucorrhœa arising from gonorrhœa ; yellow, purulent gonorrhœa ; hæmaturia ; strangury. Milky, acrid, sore-making discharge, with painful micturition. Profuse, green gonorrhœal discharge, with distressing burning and sensation of warmth in vagina, particularly when urinating. Burning red spots in vulva. Itching of vulva.

chest_heart.text: Oppression of chest, with labored breathing as if respiratory passage was filled with mucus. Pressure and anxiety in chest, with transient flushes of heat to face and burning in palms of hands and soles of feet. Burning in chest ; soreness and cough. Dyspnœa, soreness and rawness in chest and occasional stitches in left side. θ Bronchial and vesical catarrh. Mucous rales in whole chest, most in left lung ; dulness under left clavicle and want of normal resonance on whole of left side. θ Bronchial and vesical catarrh. Chronic pulmonary catarrh, with profuse expectoration of a greenish-grey, purulent mucus, of a disgusting odor, sometimes mixed with blood. Hæmoptysis. Palpitation. Heart's action tumultuous, pulse irritable and 100 per minute. θ Bronchial and vesical catarrh. Quick pulse.

skin.text: Large red blotches all over the body, with constipation and some fever. Dark-coloured or bright red, elevated, intolerable itching, lentil-sized, measle-shaped exanthema, in clusters, flowing into one another. Nettle-rash, pale red or bright red, with violent itching. Jaundice.

Jaundice. Violent itching. θ Urticaria. Itching and pricking in skin ; annoying itching with severe inflammation in parts denuded of epithelium. Chilblains. Nettlerash, isolated patches, pale red or bright red, with violent itching. Urticaria over whole body, face red ; dry, hot and biting skin (calor mordax), especially disagreeable to touch. θ Urticaria. Urticaria from gastric irritation with fever and intolerable itching. Acne of long duration, with much disfigurement of face. Papular or pustular skin eruption occurring in groups, with pain in limbs and disordered stomach. Large red blotches all over body, with constipation and some fever. Dark-colored or bright red, elevated, intolerably itching, lentil-sized, measle-shaped exanthema, in clusters, flowing one into another. Uneasiness during two days, followed by a very hard chill, which is succeeded by heat and an eruption of very circumscribed lenticular patches ; this eruption gives rise to intense itching and pricking of skin ; it is like measles, but without any catarrhal symptoms ; on third day fever subsides, and eruption grows paler ; on seventh skin has a mottled appearance ; the eruption does not wholly disappear for five days ; no desquamation. Violent chill, headache and general malaise ; red, hot skin, nettlerash all over body, delirium, drowsiness, scanty urine, which is dark, with brick-dust sediment. Eczema, consisting of small vesicles, smaller and flatter than the mercurial eczema. Tormenting eruptions only between fingers, and on both forearms as far as bend of elbow ; pustules like the itch, with red areola, containing a red, watery fluid, with itching pain, desquamation after a few days. Measles, scarlet rash, roseola ; miliary eruption ; itch.

sleep.text: Drowsiness in daytime. Restless sleep at night. Frightful, or lascivious dreams.
Drowsiness or sleepiness. θ Urticaria. Restless nights. θ Bronchial and vesical catarrh.

fever_chill.text: Quotidian fever, shiverings and cold in the forenoon, with pain in the dorsa of the feet; then, in the afternoon, general heat and thirst, with desire for cold water. During the febrile cold, the instep is painfully sensible to motion. Perspiration profuse of a pungent smell. Sour-smelling sweat at night. Profuse inodorous sweat in morning. Copyright © Médi-T ® 2000 Main.

Violent chills, with headache and general malaise. θ Nettlerash. Chilliness and coldness in forenoon, with pain in dorsa of feet ; in afternoon, heat and thirst for water. Uneasiness during two days, followed by a very hard chill, which is succeeded by heat and an eruption ; on third day, fever subsides and eruption grows paler. Fever and burning sensation, insomnia and nocturnal agitation. θ Urticaria. Fever with large red blotches over body, constipation. Perspiration profuse, of a pungent smell. Sweat somewhat relieves nausea and colic. Night sweats. θ Bronchial and vesical catarrh. Febrile disturbance during inflammatory stage of gonorrhœa.

respiratory.text: VOICE AND LARYNX. TRACHEA AND BRONCHIA. [25] [Cop.]. Dryness and roughness in larynx. Constant irritation in larynx, exciting cough. Hoarseness, especially in morning. Excoriating pain in larynx. Bronchial catarrh, soreness of chest and cough. Bronchitis, with profuse expectoration. Chronic bronchorrhœa (dilated bronchi), with profuse expectoration of greenish, purulent, fetid mucus. Oppression of chest, with labored breathing, while working in a stooping position, as when digging ; pressure on sternum ; slow respiration. Respiration quick and panting ; could breathe easily when bolstered up in bed, mucous rales in whole chest, most in left lung. θ Bronchial and vesical catarrh. Cough, with soreness of chest. Mucous cough ; cough, with shortness of breath. Dry, painful cough, with dryness in larynx and huskiness. Cough during measles. (OBS :) Chronic cough, with fistula in ano. Profuse expectoration of greenish-grey, purulent mucus, of a disgusting odor, sometimes mixed with blood. Severe, harassing cough, with profuse expectoration of thick, heavy masses of yellow or greenish and putrid tasting mucus, sometimes bloody and in such quantities as to cause choking and vomiting. θ Bronchial and vesical catarrh. Increased expectoration in phthisis pulmonalis. Spitting of blood.

extremities.text: Pain and swelling of knees and malleoli. Gonorrhœal rheumatism, especially if located in fibrous part of knee joint. (OBS :) Neuralgia of nervus pudendus internus, in consequence of gonorrhœa ; pains begin in scrotum and anus, and extend down to right side of foot, with severe cramps. Great restlessness of extremities. θ Urticaria. Pain in limbs, stomach and joints.

modalities.text: REST. POSITION. MOTION. [35] [Cop.]. When bolstered up in bed : can breathe easily. Unable to sit up : weariness, debility. Bending double : colic >. While working in stooping position : pressure on sternum, slow respiration. In morning : diarrhœa < ; white diarrheic stools, with chilliness and colic ; hoarseness ; chilliness. In afternoon : heat and thirst. At night : discharge runs down posterior nares, causing choking and other disagreeable symptoms ; greatly excited ; restless ; sweating. After catching cold : pains in neck of bladder on attempting to urinate, occasional retention of urine.
